steps.title: Spot the key contrast: impulse vs. calculated planning
steps.content: The phrase “pose so subtly considered that if one were not on the lookout one mistook it for impulse” reveals the decisive signal: Osmond's actions appear spontaneous but are actually the result of careful deliberation. This shows that “consideration” refers to calculated thinking.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Calculation expresses deliberate, premeditated thinking—the meaning that captures Osmond's calculated life. Deference, indecision, and courtesy don't fit. That matches choice B — B is correct.
